title: Error: while running runtime: exit status 127
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

New Install of Fedora 38 Server. Tried following the Buildah Tutorial 1.

When trying `buildah run $container bash` met with the error **Error: while running runtime: exit status 127**


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo dnf -y install buildah`
2. `container=$(buildah from fedora)`
3. `buildah run $container bash`


**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: while running runtime: exit status 127`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Bash prompt in the fedora-working-container


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.30.0-1.fc38.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.30.0
Go Version:      go1.20.2
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0-rc.1
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.25.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Mon Apr 10 15:26:00 2023
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.2
API Version:  4.4.2
Go Version:   go1.20.1
Built:        Wed Mar  1 19:22:39 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="38 (Server Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=38
VERSION_CODENAME=""

Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux Zee-DevBox 6.2.9-300.fc38.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Mar 30 22:32:58 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

Sorry, this file does not appear to exist. Here's the file structure.

```
cat: /etc/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory

[briggzee@Zee-DevBox ~]$ exa --tree /etc/containers/
/etc/containers
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ certs.d
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ networks

в”‚  в””в”Ђв”Ђ hooks.d

в”њв”Ђв”Ђ registries.conf

в”‚  в””в”Ђв”Ђ 000-shortnames.conf
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ registries.d
в”‚  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ default.yaml

в”‚  в””в”Ђв”Ђ registry.redhat.io.yaml
в””в”Ђв”Ђ systemd
```

comments.body: Could you please share output of same commands with flag `--log-level=debug` ? Thanks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
My apologies. I have scrapped that Fedora install and started again, and have not been able to replicate the error.
